summary: No-ship: P-1 and P-3 are addressed, but P-2’s reconciliation lock remains tautological and cannot detect the unsafe symlink regression it claims to pin.

findings.title: P-2 reconciliation characterization does not depend on the symlink
findings.body: The plan claims the committed test pins symlink traversal through both list and reconcile. However, the test leaves `b/real.meta.json` in place and makes the symlink reference the same SHA. Reconciliation therefore still reports `referenced: 1` and `gc_pending: 0` if it ignores the symlink entirely, allowing a pointers-all regression that could garbage-collect blobs referenced only through symlinked metadata. This is a tautological behavior lock at the reconciliation seam.
findings.recommendation: Make the symlink the only metadata pointer to its blob before calling `run_once`, then assert `referenced: 1`, no pending GC entry, and blob survival. Refresh the characterization baseline afterward.

next_steps: Correct the P-2 test, commit the strengthened lock, and rerun the full `cargo test` lock command in a writable environment.
